critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

1. DEMAND_RESPONSE_CONFOUND: Interruptible-load contracts with large industrial customers and TOU tariff revisions changed what the load looks like between years — a structurally easier forecasting problem.
2. NONSTATIONARY_TARGET_PROCESS: TOU tariff shifted commercial cooling behavior, altering load curve shape. The compared years are not the same forecasting problem.
3. WEATHER_INPUT_MISMATCH: Better weather vendor coverage is an input quality advantage, not a methodology advantage.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE:

The new grid environment is the one the utility must operate in. The model is built and evaluated for this environment. That said, the comparison as stated — year-over-year MAPE — does not isolate methodology from environment. Both models must be evaluated on the same historical intervals with the same weather data to establish which methodology is genuinely better. The current evidence supports: 'the total system (new model + new weather data) performs better in the new grid environment.' It does not support: 'the new modeling approach generalizes better to modern grid conditions.'
adjudication_raw: Convergence on empirical_test_agreed. Both parties agree the year-over-year comparison is not a clean methodology test. Matched historical evaluation required.

empirical_test.measure: MAPE on matched historical summer intervals, both models with identical weather inputs, separately evaluated on curtailment and non-curtailment days
empirical_test.success_criterion: New model shows no improvement on matched intervals — confirming environment and input changes explain the year-over-year gap
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model consistently improves on matched intervals under identical inputs — confirming genuine methodology advantage
